name: Fresh Gingerbread with Port-Poached Pears
recipeIngredient: 8 large Firm but ripe pears with stems
recipeInstructions:
-------------
Cake:
---------
1/4 cup Finely chopped peeled - fresh ginger
2-1/2 tablespoons Sugar
2-2/3 cups All-purpose flour
1-1/4 teaspoons Baking soda
1 tablespoon Ground ginger
2 teaspoons Ground cinnamon
3/4 teaspoon Dry mustard powder
1/4 teaspoon Ground cloves
1 cup Unsalted butter, room temperature
3/4 cup Firmly packed dark brown sugar
1/3 cup Unsulfured (light) molasses
1/3 cup Strong brewed coffee
4 Large eggs, room temperature
Cream:
-------------
1 Cup Heavy or whipping cream
2 tablespoons Powdered sugar
1/2 teaspoon Vanilla extract
1/4 teaspoon Ground ginger
For Pears: Add lemon juice to a large bowl of cold water. Peel pears, leaving stems intact. Use a melon baller to remove the core through the bottom end. Add pears to the bowl of water. Bring Port, sugar, ginger, cinnamon sticks, and vanilla bean to boil in a heavy large Dutch oven over high heat, stirring until sugar dissolves. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer syrup for 10 minutes. Drain pears and add them to the syrup. Simmer until tender, turning frequently, about 20 minutes. Using a slotted spoon, transfer pears to a large container and stand upright. Boil syrup until reduced to 1-3/4 cups, about 18 minutes. Pour syrup over pears. Cover and chill. (Can be prepared 1 day ahead.)
For Cake: Preheat oven to 350°F. Butter a 9x13x2-inch pan. Dust the pan with flour; tap out excess. Mix chopped ginger and 2-1/2 tablespoons sugar in a small bowl until the mixture forms, stirring frequently for about 30 minutes. In a medium bowl, mix flour, baking soda, and spices. Using an electric mixer, beat butter and brown sugar in a large bowl until fluffy. Add molasses, coffee, and chopped ginger mixture and beat until blended (mixture may look curdled). Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Reduce speed to low. Alternate adding dry ingredients with buttermilk, beginning and ending with dry ingredients, and beating after each addition until just combined. Transfer batter to prepared pan. Bake until a tester inserted into the center comes out clean, about 30 minutes. Transfer to a rack and cool in the pan. (Can be prepared 8 hours ahead. Cover and let stand at room temperature.)
For Cream: Beat whipping cream, powdered sugar, vanilla extract, and ground ginger in a large bowl until firm peaks form. (Can be prepared 3 hours ahead. Cover and refrigerate. Rewhip if necessary.) Cut the gingerbread into 2-1/2 inch squares. Place one cake square and one pear on each of 8 plates. Spoon some syrup over each pear. Spoon a dollop of whipped cream alongside.
